title: NoSQL injection in MongoDB node Find And Replace via unvalidated updateKey filter value
description: In the MongoDB node's Find And Replace (findOneAndReplace) operation, the match filter is built as `{ [updateKey]: item[updateKey] }` directly from the incoming item data without validating the type of the updateKey value. Because MongoDB interprets an object value as a set of query operators, an authenticated user with workflow edit access could supply an object such as `{ "$ne": null }` (or `{ "$gt": "" }`) for the updateKey field, causing the filter to match unintended documents that are then overwritten with attacker-controlled replacement content. This is a NoSQL (query/operator) injection in the document match filter.
